metadata.title: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent.
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Czech. Medhavy 1-word budget. Wagwan is Bear-only.
metadata.audio_note: All existing inner-beat mp3s must be regenerated with kokoro af_kore: python3 runtime/scripts/generate_audio.py quantum-mechanics-vol1/youtube/vox-bloch-sphere

beats.narration_text: The counting is what grabbed me: four real parameters, minus one for normalization, minus one for global phase, leaving theta and phi to place a point on the globe. I want to see the geometry work. Please: (1) show explicitly how normalization and global phase each remove one parameter, (2) take a specific state like theta equals pi over two, phi equals pi over two and tell me its x, y, and z spin measurement probabilities, and (3) explain why two states differing only in global phase land on the identical point.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: The counting is what grabbed me: four real parameters, minus one for normalization, minus one for global phase, leaving theta and phi to place a point on the globe. I want to see the geometry work. Please: (1) show explicitly how normalization and global phase each remove one parameter, (2) take a specific state like theta equals pi over two, phi equals pi over two and tell me its x, y, and z spin measurement probabilities, and (3) explain why two states differing only in global phase land on the identical point.

beats.narration_text: A quantum bit can be spin-up, spin-down, or any superposition. How do you picture all the possible superpositions at once? Every one of them is a point on the surface of a sphere.

card.copy: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: classical bit as two discrete points (0 and 1) contrasted with a qubit as a continuous sphere surface"

beats.narration_text: A qubit state has two complex amplitudes. That seems like four real parameters. But normalization fixes one, and a global phase is unobservable. That removes two more. Two real numbers remain — exactly the two angles needed to point to a spot on a sphere.
Four complex parameters. Normalization and global phase each remove one. Two free parameters. Why does that give a sphere, and what are the poles?

card.copy:
A qubit has 4 real parameters.
Normalization removes 1. Global phase removes 1.
2 remain. Why a sphere?

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: Bloch sphere with labeled north pole (|0⟩), south pole (|1⟩), and equator points showing equal superpositions"

beats.narration_text: The two remaining free parameters are the polar angle theta and the azimuthal angle phi. They parametrize the sphere exactly. The state is cosine of theta-over-two times spin-up, plus e-to-the-i-phi times sine of theta-over-two times spin-down.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: parameterize the Bloch sphere with theta and phi — show how varying theta from 0 to π sweeps from |0⟩ to |1⟩"

beats.narration_text: The north pole is theta equals zero: pure spin-up. The south pole is theta equals pi: pure spin-down. The entire equator is where theta equals pi over two — equal superpositions, differing only in relative phase.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: qubit state |+⟩ and |−⟩ on the equator of the Bloch sphere, show their relationship to the computational basis"

beats.narration_text: The azimuthal angle phi is the relative phase between spin-up and spin-down. A different phi at the same theta means a different superposition — with different measurement outcomes for x or y spin. Relative phase is physically real and distinguishable.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: single-qubit gate as rotation on the Bloch sphere — X gate flips north to south, Z gate rotates around the vertical axis"

beats.narration_text: What is the global phase? Multiplying the entire state by e-to-the-i-gamma shifts both amplitudes equally. No measurement of any observable can detect it. The Bloch sphere automatically collapses it: two states differing only in global phase map to the same point.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: measurement on the Bloch sphere — project a general state onto the north or south pole, show the probability as the cosine-squared of the angle"

beats.narration_text: The Bloch vector provides a direct sanity check. Compute the three expectation values — sigma-x, sigma-y, sigma-z. They form a vector whose length must equal exactly one for any pure state. If you compute a state and its Bloch vector has length less than one, you made an error.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: full Bloch sphere summary labeling all key states and axes — computational basis, Hadamard eigenstates, Y eigenstates"

beats.narration_text: Every physical qubit — an electron spin, a photon polarization, a superconducting circuit — has its state represented by a point on this sphere. Quantum gates move the point across the sphere's surface. The north and south poles are the computational basis states zero and one.

A qubit has four real parameters. Remove global phase and normalization — two remain: theta and phi. Two angles, one sphere. Poles are definite spin. Equator is equal superposition. Relative phase is real and distinguishable.

card.copy:
4 params minus normalization minus global phase = 2.
Theta and phi place you on a sphere.
Poles: |0> and |1>.
Equator: equal superpositions.

beats.narration_text: The sphere holds every qubit state. Paste this into Claude and navigate it.

props.segment: Why Every Qubit Lives on a Globe
props.command: claude "build a Bloch sphere visualization: show the north pole, south pole, equator states as superpositions, and animate a qubit rotating under a magnetic field"
